summary: Package exfiltrates uncaught exception stack traces to hardcoded Telegram bot on require
details: The main entry index.js, executed on require(), registers a `process.on('uncaughtException',...)` handler that forwards stringified stack traces (up to ~4090 chars) via HTTPS GET to `api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage?chat_id=<id>` (index.js:1-3, 48-53, 61-64). Stack traces from a consuming application routinely contain filesystem paths, request payloads, environment values, and occasionally secrets — all silently relayed to an author-controlled Telegram chat with no opt-in or disclosure. Additionally, loading the module installs a global `L` Proxy whose methods (log/err/inf/wrn/dir/ibb) each forward their arguments to the same Telegram endpoint, and `L.dir` additionally PUTs JSON-encoded arguments to `iiilll.firebaseio.com`. Method names mimic console logging, increasing the chance that downstream code unwittingly leaks data. This is an unambiguous installer-side data-exfiltration channel, not merely an author credential leak.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
